qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization in runner API permits attacker-controlled code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a symlink path traversal vulnerability in safe_extract_tarfile() (filesystem.py:58-96) where symlink targets are not validated during tar extraction, enabling arbitrary file write (CWE-22/CWE-59). This finding describes pickle deserialization RCE at runner_app.py:301 via pickle.loads() on untrusted HTTP request bodies (CWE-502). The root cause, vulnerable function, file, and vulnerability class are entirely different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-m6w7-qv66-g3mf) describes a symlink path traversal vulnerability in BentoML's safe_extract_tarfile() function (src/bentoml/_internal/utils/filesystem.py:58-96), where symlink targets in tar archives are not validated, enabling arbitrary file write (CWE-22/CWE-59). The sole qualifying finding 'pickle-deserialization-rce' describes a pickle deserialization RCE at a different location (runner_app.py:301, CWE-502) with a different root cause, vulnerable function, and vulnerability class. No PoC execution is warranted.
evidence: Advisory identifies safe_extract_tarfile() in src/bentoml/_internal/utils/filesystem.py:58-96 as the vulnerable function
Advisory root cause: symlink target not validated during tar extraction, allowing arbitrary file write via symlink path traversal
Advisory CWE: CWE-22 (Path Traversal), CWE-59 (Improper Link Resolution Before File Access)
Finding pickle-deserialization-rce targets runner_app.py:301 with pickle.loads() on HTTP request bodies — different function, file, and vulnerability class (CWE-502)
